rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. JI JIN ZHU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 30, 2024, Line 1. PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for entry of judgment pursuant to stipulation of the parties" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: TRUCONNECT COMMUNICATIONS, INC. VS. MAXIMUS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S April 23, 2024 Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 30, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF TRUCONNECT COMMUNICATIONS, INC.'s Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S April 23, 2024 Orders. Plaintiff's "motion for reconsideration of the court's April 23, 2024 orders" on three pleadings motions is denied. California law requires a meet-and-confer before a pleadings motion at which the party that filed the pleading "shall provide legal support for its position that the pleading is legally sufficient or, in the alternative, how" the pleading "could be amended to cure any legal insufficiency." (CCP 430.41(a)(1), 435.5(a)(1).) It was clear from counsel's declaration filed with the three motions that the law was not followed here (2/13/24 Perez Dec. 1:14-20) - and plaintiff's opposition papers on the three motions did not claim it was. Thus, the court's tentative rulings posted April 22 (1) pointed out the illegality, (2) took the pleadings motions off calendar so the parties could comply with the meet-and-confer laws and (3) set a new date of May 23 for responsive papers. On April 23, the tentatives became the court's orders after no one contested them. Plaintiff provides no valid reason for not presenting its arguments earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) In any event, plaintiff's untimely arguments are unavailing. It is not the parties' role to decide when the meet-and-confer laws have been followed, it is the court's. (See Memo. 7:8-12.) Plaintiff had a "meaningful opportunity" to address the meet-and-confer at the April 23 hearing, but elected not to do so. (See id. at 7:13.) Plaintiff is not suffering "extraordinary prejudice" from the May 23 response date (see id. at 8:2-3); if the parties could not resolve their pleadings disputes in a compliant meet-and-confer (as they should), a hearing can be scheduled in mid-June - a few days' difference in a case filed in 2021. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MORROW-MEADOWS CORPORATION, VS. SOMA HOTEL LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Challenge Soma Hotel Llc'S Claim Of Privilege Aas To Docs Produced By Non-Party Deponents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May-30-2024, Line 3, 1-CROSS DEFENDANT HATHAWAY DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's Motion To Challenge Soma Hotel Llc'S Claim Of Privilege As To Docs Produced By Non-Party Deponents. Pro Tem Judge Geri Green, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. For the 1:30 p.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 302 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Discovery Department 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to gerilynngreen@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN MATCKE CROSBY VS. BETHANIA BACIGALUPE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Keller, Fishback & Jaccion Llp For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 30, 2024, Line 14. DEFENDANT KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP's Motion For Attorney'S Fees. Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP's motion for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $38,772. "The experienced trial judge is the best judge of the value of professional services rendered in his court." (Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49.) A $1,382 hourly rate for debt collection work is unreasonable; half that is awarded ($691). The time reportedly spent on counsel's work is reasonable. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: F.D. THOMAS, INC. AN OREGON CORPORATION VS. SHIMMICK/DANNY'S JOINT VENTURE, A CALIFORNIA JOINT ET A
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 30, 2024, Line 12. 2 - DEFENDANT SHIMMICK/DANNY'S JOINT VENTURE's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Michael McNamara's unopposed pro hac vice application is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 30, 2024, Line 13. 1 - DEFENDANT SOLANA LABS, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration. Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. The parties never entered into an arbitration agreement (including the delegation clause.) Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
